name: Pico de los Reales Trail
name_original: La subida del Pico de los Reales — ida y vuelta — El Parque Natural Los Reales de Sierra Bermeja
description: Hike the moderate 2.3-mile Pico de los Reales Trail for panoramic views of the Mediterranean and unique pinsapo forests in Sierra Bermeja.
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja traverse a landscape defined by its unique reddish peridotite rocks and extensive Spanish fir forests. The region offers panoramic views of the Costa del Sol, the Genal Valley, and the Strait of Gibraltar. Hikers can explore a diverse network of paths, from river routes to ascents towards peaks like Pico Los Reales. The area's distinct geology supports specialized flora and fauna, making it a notable destination for outdoor exploration. Best dog friendly hiking trails in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja The most popular dog friendly hiking route is Genalguacil Village – Lentisco Viewpoint loop from Genalguacil, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views over the Genal Valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Angel Aqueduct – Waterfront Pathway in Spain loop from Estepona, an easy 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route follows a waterfront pathway with minimal elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Picturesque Country Cottage – The Old Mill loop from Jubrique, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja is defined by unique peridotite rock formations, extensive Spanish fir forests, and panoramic coastal views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate ascents. The routes in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 900 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja offers a selection of over 10 dog-friendly hiking trails. These routes vary in difficulty, with 5 easy and 7 moderate options, ensuring there's something suitable for most dogs and their owners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on dog-friendly hikes in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: The terrain in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja is quite unique, characterized by its reddish peridotite rocks, which give the mountains their distinctive 'vermilion' color. You'll find paths winding through rare Spanish fir forests (pinsapares) and potentially rocky, mountainous sections. Some trails, like those leading to higher peaks, can be steep, while others offer more accessible, gentle slopes.

Q: Are there any easy dog-friendly trails suitable for beginners or older dogs?
A: Yes, there are several easy dog-friendly options. For a gentle coastal walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Angel Aqueduct – Waterfront Pathway in Spain loop from Estepona&lt;/a&gt;, which is mostly flat. While not explicitly listed as easy, the region also features circular routes like the 'Reales de Sierra Bermeja trail' which is generally well-signposted and suitable for all ages, offering a good option for a relaxed outing with your dog.

Q: Are there any circular dog-friendly walks in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the dog-friendly trails in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31562972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genalguacil Village – Lentisco Viewpoint loop from Genalguacil&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge and beautiful scenery.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see with my dog?
A: The region is famous for its unique Spanish fir forests, known as pinsapares. You can explore these ancient woodlands on trails like the 'Paseo de los Pinsapos' or the 'Reales de Sierra Bermeja trail'. For stunning vistas, head towards viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1120392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvador Guerrero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, where on clear days you might even see the Strait of Gibraltar and the Moroccan coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de los Reales — Summit View&lt;/a&gt; also offers breathtaking panoramas.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: The mild climate of Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja makes it suitable for hiking with dogs for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant flora. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable, especially on more exposed trails. Winters are generally mild, but higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for hiking with dogs in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: While specific permits for hiking with dogs are generally not required, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks and protected areas like the pinsapar forests, to protect local wildlife and flora. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local signage regarding pet access.

Q: Where can I park when bringing my dog for a hike in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many villages like Genalguacil and Jubrique, which serve as starting points for several trails, offer public parking. For routes closer to the coast, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31830319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marbella Beach Promenade – Estepona Seafront Promenade loop from Estepona&lt;/a&gt;, you'll find parking options within the towns themselves.

Q: What should I bring for a dog-friendly hike in this region?
A: Beyond your usual hiking essentials, remember to pack plenty of water for both yourself and your dog, especially given the region's climate. A portable water bowl, dog treats, waste bags, and a leash are crucial. Depending on the trail, dog booties might be useful for rocky sections. Always check the weather forecast and dress appropriately for both of you.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about dog-friendly trails in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Los Reales De Sierra Bermeja, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique natural beauty, particularly the ancient pinsapar forests, and the stunning panoramic views that stretch from the Costa del Sol to the Strait of Gibraltar. The diverse trail network, offering both challenging and relaxed options, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails near the coast or with water features?
A: Yes, you can find dog-friendly trails that offer coastal views or follow river systems. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31830319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marbella Beach Promenade – Estepona Seafront Promenade loop from Estepona&lt;/a&gt; provides a pleasant walk along the Mediterranean coast. While not explicitly listed in the routes, the region's research mentions routes along rivers like Rio Padron, which can feature refreshing pools, offering a chance for your dog to cool off.
tips: A designated observation viewpoint on the climb to Pico de los Reales on the Sierra Bermeja mountain. Spectacular views of the bay of Estepona. Now, can you reach the top?

The 3.1km climb rewards you with spectacular views. From the peak of Los Reales, the highest peak in Sierra Bermeja with 1,452 meters above sea level, the views are impressive. It is best climbed on clear days, where you can even see the peaks of the Sierra Nevada.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pico de los Reales Trail?
faqs.answer: There is a parking lot conveniently located near the summit of Pico de los Reales. The road leading up to the summit was repaved recently and is in excellent condition, though it does have narrow sections and hairpin turns. This makes accessing the trailhead by car quite straightforward.

faqs.question: Is the Pico de los Reales Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Natural Park Los Reales de Sierra Bermeja generally allows dogs, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like Iberian ibex. Please ensure you follow all local regulations and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Pico de los Reales Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning scenery year-round. However, for the most comfortable hiking experience and clearer views, spring and autumn are often ideal. During these seasons, temperatures are milder than the summer heat, and the risk of fog at the summit might be lower than in winter. Be aware that summit temperatures can be 6 to 10 degrees Celsius lower than on the coast.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Natural Park Los Reales de Sierra Bermeja?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for general hiking access to the Natural Park Los Reales de Sierra Bermeja. However, it's always a good idea to check for any specific regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit, especially if you plan to visit during peak times or engage in specific activities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter unpaved, gravelly, and stony paths, as well as some paved sections and rocky firebreaks. Hikers should be prepared for irregular surfaces and uneven steps. Sturdy shoes are highly recommended for sure-footedness, especially on the more challenging sections.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers incredible panoramic views. You'll pass by the Salvador Guerrero Viewpoint, which provides 180-degree views of the coastline. From the Pico de los Reales — Summit View, you can see the Mediterranean Sea, the Strait of Gibraltar, the Rock of Gibraltar, and even the African coastline on clear days. The unique pinsapo forest is also a major highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Pico de los Reales Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The difficulty of routes to Pico de los Reales can range from moderate to hard. The specific route highlighted, "The ascent of Pico de los Reales — round trip," is considered moderate, covering about 3.77 km with 180m of elevation gain and taking around 1 hour and 17 minutes. While it requires good fitness, there are options suitable for various skill levels. Longer or steeper routes demand very good fitness and alpine experience.

faqs.question: What unique wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Natural Park is renowned for its unique vegetation, particularly one of the largest Spanish fir (pinsapo) forests in the world, which grows on rare peridotite rocks. You might also spot Iberian ibex (mountain goats), especially during early morning or late afternoon near the refuge.

faqs.question: Is the Pico de los Reales Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The specific route highlighted, "The ascent of Pico de los Reales — round trip," is a loop trail. There are also other circular options available in the area, such as "Pico Los Reales from La Loma del Infierno."

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the summit?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the summit, you'll find a belvedere (viewpoint), a parking lot, and even a small bar, providing some conveniences for visitors. These amenities make the summit area a comfortable spot to rest and enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers 100% of the official trail named "Subida al Pico Los Reales." It also intersects with sections of other trails such as "[CIMA ML17] Peñas Blancas * Jubrique" and "[CIMA ML17] Peñas Blancas * Los Reales."

waypoints.name: Pico de los Reales — Summit View
